meta_title: For Freelancers: API integration guide to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: End Project Stalls with API Integration
content.seo_description: Stop leaving projects unfinished. Master API integration with Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver faster, high-value client work as a freelancer.
content.answer_block: The secret to finishing projects is reducing friction through automated API integration. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for intelligent execution, you bypass manual boilerplate, minimize debugging time, and ship production-ready features in hours, not weeks. Focus on the value, not the plumbing.
content.intro:
The graveyard of freelance careers is littered with half-finished projects. You start with high energy, but once the architecture gets complex and the documentation for a third-party API looks like a labyrinth, momentum dies. You aren't failing because you lack skill; you are failing because you are building from scratch in an era where you should be orchestrating.

It is time to stop wrestling with syntax and start shipping. By mastering the synergy between Antigravity and Claude Code, you transform from a manual coder into a high-velocity AI architect. This workflow doesn't just save time; it creates a repeatable system that turns daunting integrations into predictable, plug-and-play modules.

steps.title: Define the API Contract
steps.description: Before writing code, establish a clear schema. Use AI to parse the API documentation and create a structured interface that prevents runtime errors later.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the provided API documentation URL and generate a TypeScript interface for the primary endpoints, including request/response schemas and error handling structures.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a new file named api-schema.ts based on the docs provided'

steps.title: Scaffold the Integration Layer
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate service layer. This ensures your project follows clean architecture patterns immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a robust API service module using Axios, implementing interceptors for automatic token injection and structured logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a service class in src/services/api.ts that implements the schema defined in step 1'

steps.title: Implement Intelligent Error Handling
steps.description: Don't just catch errors; handle them. Create a standardized response wrapper so your UI layer never crashes when an API call fails.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a centralized error handler that maps API error codes to user-friendly messages and logs the raw trace for debugging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Wrap all API methods in a generic error handler that returns a standardized result type'

steps.title: Mocking for Rapid Development
steps.description: Don't wait for your backend to be ready. Use Claude Code to create local mock servers so your frontend development continues uninterrupted.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a MSW (Mock Service Worker) handler file that mirrors the API endpoints defined in the schema for local testing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a mocks folder and implement MSW handlers for all endpoints'

steps.title: Automated Integration Testing
steps.description: Verify your integration with automated tests that run every time you push code, ensuring your client's project remains stable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of integration tests for the API service layer using Vitest, covering success paths and common failure scenarios.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate unit tests for api.ts ensuring 100% path coverage'

pro_tips: Always use environment variables for keys; never hardcode credentials, even in development, to avoid accidental leaks.
Implement request caching at the service level to reduce API costs and improve UX for repeat data fetches.
Use Claude Code to perform a 'security audit' on your generated code before deployment to catch potential injection points.
Tag your API logs with correlation IDs to trace requests across services, making production debugging 10x faster.
Build a library of 'Integration Blueprints'—save your successful Antigravity prompts to reuse for future client projects.
content.next_steps: Once your API layer is functional, integrate it into your UI components using React Query to handle state synchronization, then deploy your MVP to Vercel.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to stop stalled projects and start delivering high-value, production-ready software for your clients. Don't let another project languish in your drafts folder. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
